commands.description: Set expiration for hash fields using relative time to expire (milliseconds)
commands.syntax:
HPEXPIRE key milliseconds [NX | XX | GT | LT] FIELDS numfields field
  [field ...]
commands.title: HPEXPIRE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields
commands.description: Set expiration for hash fields using an absolute Unix timestamp (milliseconds)
commands.syntax:
HPEXPIREAT key unix-time-milliseconds [NX | XX | GT | LT]
  FIELDS numfields field [field ...]
commands.title: HPEXPIREAT

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields
commands.description: Returns the expiration time of a hash field as a Unix timestamp, in msec.
commands.title: HPEXPIRETIME

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields
commands.description: Returns the TTL in milliseconds of a hash field.
commands.title: HPTTL

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields returned
commands.description: Returns one or more random fields from a hash.
commands.title: HRANDFIELD

commands.complexity: O(1) for every call. O(N) for a complete iteration, including enough command calls for the cursor to return back to 0. N is the number of elements inside the collection.
commands.description: Iterates over fields and values of a hash.
commands.title: HSCAN

commands.complexity: O(1) for each field/value pair added, so O(N) to add N field/value pairs when the command is called with multiple field/value pairs.
commands.description: Creates or modifies the value of a field in a hash.
commands.title: HSET

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields being set.
commands.description: Set the value of one or more fields of a given hash key, and optionally set their expiration.
commands.syntax:
HSETEX key [FNX | FXX] [EX seconds | PX milliseconds |
  EXAT unix-time-seconds | PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | KEEPTTL]
  FIELDS numfields field value [field value ...]
commands.title: HSETEX

commands.description: Sets the value of a field in a hash only when the field doesn't exist.
commands.title: HSETNX

commands.description: Returns the length of the value of a field.
commands.title: HSTRLEN

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of specified fields
commands.description: Returns the TTL in seconds of a hash field.
commands.title: HTTL

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the size of the hash.
commands.description: Returns all values in a hash.
commands.title: HVALS

commands.complexity: O(1) to add every element.
commands.description: Adds elements to a HyperLogLog key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: PFADD

commands.complexity: O(1) with a very small average constant time when called with a single key. O(N) with N being the number of keys, and much bigger constant times, when called with multiple keys.
commands.description: Returns the approximated cardinality of the set(s) observed by the HyperLogLog key(s).
commands.title: PFCOUNT

commands.complexity: N/A
commands.description: Internal commands for debugging HyperLogLog values.
commands.title: PFDEBUG

commands.complexity: O(N) to merge N HyperLogLogs, but with high constant times.
commands.description: Merges one or more HyperLogLog values into a single key.
commands.title: PFMERGE

commands.complexity: N/A
commands.description: An internal command for testing HyperLogLog values.
commands.title: PFSELFTEST

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Append one or more JSON values into the array at path after the last element in it.
commands.title: JSON.ARRAPPEND

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of a JSON scalar value in the array at path
commands.title: JSON.ARRINDEX

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Inserts the JSON scalar(s) value at the specified index in the array at path
commands.title: JSON.ARRINSERT

commands.complexity: O(1) where path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) where path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Returns the length of the array at path
commands.title: JSON.ARRLEN

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array and the specified index is not the last element, O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value and the specified index is the last element, or O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Removes and returns the element at the specified index in the array at path
commands.title: JSON.ARRPOP

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the array, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Trims the array at path to contain only the specified inclusive range of indices from start to stop
commands.title: JSON.ARRTRIM

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the values, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Clears all values from an array or an object and sets numeric values to `0`
commands.title: JSON.CLEAR

commands.complexity: N/A
commands.description: Debugging container command
commands.title: JSON.DEBUG

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value, where N is the size of the value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Reports the size in bytes of a key
commands.title: JSON.DEBUG MEMORY

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the deleted value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Deletes a value
commands.title: JSON.DEL

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the deleted value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Deletes a value
commands.title: JSON.FORGET

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value where N is the size of the value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Gets the value at one or more paths in JSON serialized form
commands.title: JSON.GET

commands.complexity: O(M+N) when path is evaluated to a single value where M is the size of the original value (if it exists) and N is the size of the new value, O(M+N) when path is evaluated to multiple values where M is the size of the key and N is the size of the new value * the number of original values in the key
commands.description: Merges a given JSON value into matching paths. Consequently, JSON values at matching paths are updated, deleted, or expanded with new children
commands.title: JSON.MERGE

commands.complexity: O(M*N) when path is evaluated to a single value where M is the number of keys and N is the size of the value, O(N1+N2+...+Nm) when path is evaluated to multiple values where m is the number of keys and Ni is the size of the i-th key
commands.description: Returns the values at a path from one or more keys
commands.title: JSON.MGET

commands.complexity: O(K*(M+N)) where k is the number of keys in the command, when path is evaluated to a single value where M is the size of the original value (if it exists) and N is the size of the new value, or O(K*(M+N)) when path is evaluated to multiple values where M is the size of the key and N is the size of the new value * the number of original values in the key
commands.description: Sets or updates the JSON value of one or more keys
commands.syntax: JSON.MSET key path value [key path value ...]
commands.title: JSON.MSET

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Increments the numeric value at path by a value
commands.title: JSON.NUMINCRBY

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Multiplies the numeric value at path by a value
commands.title: JSON.NUMMULTBY

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value, where N is the number of keys in the object, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Returns the JSON keys of the object at path
commands.title: JSON.OBJKEYS

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Returns the number of keys of the object at path
commands.title: JSON.OBJLEN

commands.complexity: O(N) when path is evaluated to a single value, where N is the size of the value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Returns the JSON value at path in Redis Serialization Protocol (RESP)
commands.title: JSON.RESP

commands.complexity: O(M+N) when path is evaluated to a single value where M is the size of the original value (if it exists) and N is the size of the new value, O(M+N) when path is evaluated to multiple values where M is the size of the key and N is the size of the new value * the number of original values in the key
commands.description: Sets or updates the JSON value at a path
commands.title: JSON.SET

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Appends a string to a JSON string value at path
commands.title: JSON.STRAPPEND

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Returns the length of the JSON String at path in key
commands.title: JSON.STRLEN

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Toggles a boolean value
commands.title: JSON.TOGGLE

commands.complexity: O(1) when path is evaluated to a single value, O(N) when path is evaluated to multiple values, where N is the size of the key
commands.description: Returns the type of the JSON value at path
commands.title: JSON.TYPE

commands.description: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.
commands.syntax: BLMOVE source destination <LEFT | RIGHT> <LEFT | RIGHT> timeout
commands.title: BLMOVE

commands.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the number of provided keys and M is the number of elements returned.
commands.description: Pops the first element from one of multiple lists. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.syntax: BLMPOP timeout numkeys key [key ...] <LEFT | RIGHT> [COUNT count]
commands.title: BLMPOP

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of provided keys.
commands.description: Removes and returns the first element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.title: BLPOP

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of provided keys.
commands.description: Removes and returns the last element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.title: BRPOP

commands.description: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Block until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.title: BRPOPLPUSH

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements to traverse to get to the element at index. This makes asking for the first or the last element of the list O(1).
commands.description: Returns an element from a list by its index.
commands.title: LINDEX

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements to traverse before seeing the value pivot. This means that inserting somewhere on the left end on the list (head) can be considered O(1) and inserting somewhere on the right end (tail) is O(N).
commands.description: Inserts an element before or after another element in a list.
commands.title: LINSERT

commands.description: Returns the length of a list.
commands.title: LLEN

commands.description: Returns an element after popping it from one list and pushing it to another. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.
commands.title: LMOVE

commands.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the number of provided keys and M is the number of elements returned.
commands.description: Returns multiple elements from a list after removing them. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.title: LMPOP

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements returned
commands.description: Returns the first elements in a list after removing it. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.title: LPOP

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements in the list, for the average case. When searching for elements near the head or the tail of the list, or when the MAXLEN option is provided, the command may run in constant time.
commands.description: Returns the index of matching elements in a list.
commands.syntax: LPOS key element [RANK rank] [COUNT num-matches] [MAXLEN len]
commands.title: LPOS

commands.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.
commands.description: Prepends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: LPUSH

commands.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.
commands.description: Prepends one or more elements to a list only when the list exists.
commands.title: LPUSHX

commands.complexity: O(S+N) where S is the distance of start offset from HEAD for small lists, from nearest end (HEAD or TAIL) for large lists; and N is the number of elements in the specified range.
commands.description: Returns a range of elements from a list.
commands.title: LRANGE

commands.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the length of the list and M is the number of elements removed.
commands.description: Removes elements from a list. Deletes the list if the last element was removed.
commands.title: LREM

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the list. Setting either the first or the last element of the list is O(1).
commands.description: Sets the value of an element in a list by its index.
commands.title: LSET

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements to be removed by the operation.
commands.description: Removes elements from both ends a list. Deletes the list if all elements were trimmed.
commands.title: LTRIM

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements returned
commands.description: Returns and removes the last elements of a list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.title: RPOP

commands.description: Returns the last element of a list after removing and pushing it to another list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.
commands.title: RPOPLPUSH

commands.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.
commands.description: Appends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: RPUSH

commands.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.
commands.description: Appends an element to a list only when the list exists.
commands.title: RPUSHX

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of patterns to subscribe to.
commands.description: Listens for messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.
commands.title: PSUBSCRIBE

commands.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the number of clients subscribed to the receiving channel and M is the total number of subscribed patterns (by any client).
commands.description: Posts a message to a channel.
commands.title: PUBLISH

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of active channels, and assuming constant time pattern matching (relatively short channels and patterns)
commands.description: Returns the active channels.
commands.title: PUBSUB CHANNELS

commands.description: Returns a count of unique pattern subscriptions.
commands.title: PUBSUB NUMPAT

commands.complexity: O(N) for the NUMSUB subcommand, where N is the number of requested channels
commands.description: Returns a count of subscribers to channels.
commands.title: PUBSUB NUMSUB

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of active shard channels, and assuming constant time pattern matching (relatively short shard channels).
commands.description: Returns the active shard channels.
commands.title: PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS

commands.complexity: O(N) for the SHARDNUMSUB subcommand, where N is the number of requested shard channels
commands.description: Returns the count of subscribers of shard channels.
commands.title: PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of patterns to unsubscribe.
commands.description: Stops listening to messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.
commands.title: PUNSUBSCRIBE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of clients subscribed to the receiving shard channel.
commands.description: Post a message to a shard channel
commands.title: SPUBLISH

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of shard channels to subscribe to.
commands.description: Listens for messages published to shard channels.
commands.title: SSUBSCRIBE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of channels to subscribe to.
commands.description: Listens for messages published to channels.
commands.title: SUBSCRIBE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of shard channels to unsubscribe.
commands.description: Stops listening to messages posted to shard channels.
commands.title: SUNSUBSCRIBE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of channels to unsubscribe.
commands.description: Stops listening to messages posted to channels.
commands.title: UNSUBSCRIBE

commands.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.
commands.description: Executes a server-side Lua script.
commands.title: EVAL

commands.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.
commands.description: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script.
commands.title: EVAL_RO

commands.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.
commands.description: Executes a server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.
commands.title: EVALSHA

commands.complexity: Depends on the script that is executed.
commands.description: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.
commands.title: EVALSHA_RO

commands.complexity: Depends on the function that is executed.
commands.description: Invokes a function.
commands.title: FCALL

commands.complexity: Depends on the function that is executed.
commands.description: Invokes a read-only function.
commands.title: FCALL_RO

commands.description: Deletes a library and its functions.
commands.title: FUNCTION DELETE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of functions
commands.description: Dumps all libraries into a serialized binary payload.
commands.title: FUNCTION DUMP

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of functions deleted
commands.description: Deletes all libraries and functions.
commands.title: FUNCTION FLUSH

commands.description: Terminates a function during execution.
commands.title: FUNCTION KILL

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of functions
commands.description: Returns information about all libraries.
commands.title: FUNCTION LIST

commands.complexity: O(1) (considering compilation time is redundant)
commands.description: Creates a library.
commands.title: FUNCTION LOAD

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of functions on the payload
commands.description: Restores all libraries from a payload.
commands.title: FUNCTION RESTORE

commands.description: Returns information about a function during execution.
commands.title: FUNCTION STATS

commands.description: Sets the debug mode of server-side Lua scripts.
commands.title: SCRIPT DEBUG

commands.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of scripts to check (so checking a single script is an O(1) operation).
commands.description: Determines whether server-side Lua scripts exist in the script cache.
commands.title: SCRIPT EXISTS

commands.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of scripts in cache
commands.description: Removes all server-side Lua scripts from the script cache.
commands.title: SCRIPT FLUSH

commands.description: Terminates a server-side Lua script during execution.
commands.title: SCRIPT KILL

commands.complexity: O(N) with N being the length in bytes of the script body.
commands.description: Loads a server-side Lua script to the script cache.
commands.title: SCRIPT LOAD

commands.description: Returns a list of all existing indexes
commands.title: FT._LIST

commands.description: Run a search query on an index and perform aggregate transformations on the results
commands.syntax:
FT.AGGREGATE index query [VERBATIM] [LOAD count field [field ...]]
  [TIMEOUT timeout] [LOAD *] [GROUPBY nargs property [property ...]
  [REDUCE function nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] [REDUCE function
  nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] ...]] [GROUPBY nargs property
  [property ...] [REDUCE function nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name]
  [REDUCE function nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] ...]] ...]]
  [SORTBY nargs [property <ASC | DESC> [property <ASC | DESC> ...]]
  [MAX num]] [APPLY expression AS name [APPLY expression AS name
  ...]] [LIMIT offset num] [FILTER filter] [WITHCURSOR
  [COUNT read_size] [MAXIDLE idle_time]] [PARAMS nargs name value
  [name value ...]] [DIALECT dialect]
commands.title: FT.AGGREGATE

commands.description: Adds an alias to the index
commands.title: FT.ALIASADD

commands.description: Deletes an alias from the index
commands.title: FT.ALIASDEL

commands.description: Adds or updates an alias to the index
commands.title: FT.ALIASUPDATE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys in the keyspace
commands.description: Adds a new field to the index
commands.syntax: FT.ALTER index [SKIPINITIALSCAN] SCHEMA ADD field options
commands.title: FT.ALTER

commands.description: Retrieves runtime configuration options
commands.title: FT.CONFIG GET

commands.description: Sets runtime configuration options
commands.title: FT.CONFIG SET

commands.complexity: O(K) at creation where K is the number of fields, O(N) if scanning the keyspace is triggered, where N is the number of keys in the keyspace
commands.description: Creates an index with the given spec
commands.syntax:
FT.CREATE index [ON <HASH | JSON>] [PREFIX count prefix [prefix
  ...]] [FILTER filter] [LANGUAGE default_lang]
  [LANGUAGE_FIELD lang_attribute] [SCORE default_score]
  [SCORE_FIELD score_attribute] [PAYLOAD_FIELD payload_attribute]
  [MAXTEXTFIELDS] [TEMPORARY seconds] [NOOFFSETS] [NOHL] [NOFIELDS]
  [NOFREQS] [STOPWORDS count [stopword [stopword ...]]]
  [SKIPINITIALSCAN] [INDEXALL <ENABLE | DISABLE>] SCHEMA field_name [AS alias] <TEXT | TAG |
  NUMERIC | GEO | VECTOR> [WITHSUFFIXTRIE] [INDEXEMPTY]
  [INDEXMISSING] [SORTABLE [UNF]] [NOINDEX] [field_name [AS alias]
  <TEXT | TAG | NUMERIC | GEO | VECTOR> [WITHSUFFIXTRIE]
  [INDEXEMPTY] [INDEXMISSING] [SORTABLE [UNF]] [NOINDEX] ...]
commands.title: FT.CREATE

commands.description: Deletes a cursor
commands.title: FT.CURSOR DEL

commands.description: Reads from a cursor
commands.syntax: FT.CURSOR READ index cursor_id [COUNT read size]
commands.title: FT.CURSOR READ

commands.description: Adds terms to a dictionary
commands.title: FT.DICTADD

commands.description: Deletes terms from a dictionary
commands.title: FT.DICTDEL

commands.complexity: O(N), where N is the size of the dictionary
commands.description: Dumps all terms in the given dictionary
commands.title: FT.DICTDUMP

commands.complexity: O(1) or O(N) if documents are deleted, where N is the number of keys in the keyspace
commands.description: Deletes the index
commands.title: FT.DROPINDEX

commands.description: Returns the execution plan for a complex query
commands.title: FT.EXPLAIN

commands.description: Returns the execution plan for a complex query
commands.title: FT.EXPLAINCLI

commands.complexity: O(N+M) where N is the complexity of the text search and M is the complexity of the vector search
commands.description: Performs hybrid search combining text search and vector similarity search
commands.syntax:
FT.HYBRID index
  SEARCH query
    [SCORER scorer]
    [YIELD_SCORE_AS name]
  VSIM vector_field $vector_param
    [KNN count K k [EF_RUNTIME ef_runtime]]
    [RANGE count RADIUS radius [EPSILON epsilon]]
    [YIELD_SCORE_AS name]
    [FILTER filter]
  [COMBINE RRF count [CONSTANT constant] [WINDOW window] [YIELD_SCORE_AS name]]
  [COMBINE LINEAR count [[ALPHA alpha] [BETA beta]] [WINDOW window] [YIELD_SCORE_AS name]]
  [LIMIT offset num]
  [SORTBY count sortby [ASC | DESC]]
  [NOSORT]
  [LOAD count field [field ...]]
  [LOAD *]
  [GROUPBY nargs property [property ...]
  [GROUPBY nargs property [property ...]
    [REDUCE function nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name]
    [REDUCE function nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] ...]] ...]]
  [APPLY expression AS name [APPLY expression AS name ...]]
  [FILTER filter]
  PARAMS nargs vector_param vector_blob [name value ...]
  [TIMEOUT timeout]
commands.title: FT.HYBRID

commands.description: Returns information and statistics on the index
commands.title: FT.INFO

commands.complexity: O(N)
commands.description: Performs a `FT.SEARCH` or `FT.AGGREGATE` command and collects performance information
commands.title: FT.PROFILE

commands.complexity: O(N)
commands.description: Searches the index with a textual query, returning either documents or just ids
commands.syntax:
FT.SEARCH index query [NOCONTENT] [VERBATIM] [NOSTOPWORDS]
  [WITHSCORES] [WITHPAYLOADS] [WITHSORTKEYS] [FILTER numeric_field
  min max [FILTER numeric_field min max ...]] [GEOFILTER geo_field
  lon lat radius <m | km | mi | ft> [GEOFILTER geo_field lon lat
  radius <m | km | mi | ft> ...]] [INKEYS count key [key ...]]
  [INFIELDS count field [field ...]] [RETURN count identifier
  [AS property] [identifier [AS property] ...]] [SUMMARIZE
  [FIELDS count field [field ...]] [FRAGS num] [LEN fragsize]
  [SEPARATOR separator]] [HIGHLIGHT [FIELDS count field [field ...]]
  [TAGS open close]] [SLOP slop] [TIMEOUT timeout] [INORDER]
  [LANGUAGE language] [EXPANDER expander] [SCORER scorer]
  [EXPLAINSCORE] [PAYLOAD payload] [SORTBY sortby [ASC | DESC]]
  [LIMIT offset num] [PARAMS nargs name value [name value ...]]
  [DIALECT dialect]
commands.title: FT.SEARCH

commands.description: Performs spelling correction on a query, returning suggestions for misspelled terms
commands.syntax:
FT.SPELLCHECK index query [DISTANCE distance] [TERMS <INCLUDE |
  EXCLUDE> dictionary [terms [terms ...]]] [DIALECT dialect]
commands.title: FT.SPELLCHECK

commands.description: Dumps the contents of a synonym group
commands.title: FT.SYNDUMP

commands.description: Creates or updates a synonym group with additional terms
commands.title: FT.SYNUPDATE

commands.complexity: O(N)
commands.description: Returns the distinct tags indexed in a Tag field
commands.title: FT.TAGVALS

commands.complexity: O(1) since the categories and commands are a fixed set.
commands.description: Lists the ACL categories, or the commands inside a category.
commands.title: ACL CAT

commands.complexity: O(1) amortized time considering the typical user.
commands.description: Deletes ACL users, and terminates their connections.
commands.title: ACL DELUSER

commands.description: Simulates the execution of a command by a user, without executing the command.
commands.title: ACL DRYRUN

commands.description: Generates a pseudorandom, secure password that can be used to identify ACL users.
commands.title: ACL GENPASS

commands.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of password, command and pattern rules that the user has.
commands.description: Lists the ACL rules of a user.
commands.title: ACL GETUSER

commands.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.
commands.description: Dumps the effective rules in ACL file format.
commands.title: ACL LIST

commands.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.
commands.description: Reloads the rules from the configured ACL file.
commands.title: ACL LOAD

commands.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of entries shown.
commands.description: Lists recent security events generated due to ACL rules.
commands.title: ACL LOG

commands.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.
commands.description: Saves the effective ACL rules in the configured ACL file.
commands.title: ACL SAVE

commands.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of rules provided.
commands.description: Creates and modifies an ACL user and its rules.
commands.title: ACL SETUSER

commands.complexity: O(N). Where N is the number of configured users.
commands.description: Lists all ACL users.
commands.title: ACL USERS

commands.description: Returns the authenticated username of the current connection.
commands.title: ACL WHOAMI

commands.description: Asynchronously rewrites the append-only file to disk.
commands.title: BGREWRITEAOF

commands.description: Asynchronously saves the database(s) to disk.
commands.title: BGSAVE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of Redis commands
commands.description: Returns detailed information about all commands.
commands.title: COMMAND

commands.description: Returns a count of commands.
commands.title: COMMAND COUNT

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of commands to look up
commands.description: Returns documentary information about one, multiple or all commands.
commands.title: COMMAND DOCS

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of arguments to the command
commands.description: Extracts the key names from an arbitrary command.
commands.title: COMMAND GETKEYS

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of arguments to the command
commands.description: Extracts the key names and access flags for an arbitrary command.
commands.title: COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of commands to look up
commands.description: Returns information about one, multiple or all commands.
commands.title: COMMAND INFO

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of Redis commands
commands.description: Returns a list of command names.
commands.syntax:
COMMAND LIST [FILTERBY <MODULE module-name | ACLCAT category |
  PATTERN pattern>]
commands.title: COMMAND LIST

commands.complexity: O(N) when N is the number of configuration parameters provided
commands.description: Returns the effective values of configuration parameters.
commands.title: CONFIG GET

commands.description: Resets the server's statistics.
commands.title: CONFIG RESETSTAT

commands.description: Persists the effective configuration to file.
commands.title: CONFIG REWRITE

commands.complexity: O(N) when N is the number of configuration parameters provided
commands.description: Sets configuration parameters in-flight.
commands.title: CONFIG SET

commands.description: Returns the number of keys in the database.
commands.title: DBSIZE

commands.description: Starts a coordinated failover from a server to one of its replicas.
commands.title: FAILOVER

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of keys in all databases
commands.description: Removes all keys from all databases.
commands.title: FLUSHALL

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys in the selected database
commands.description: Remove all keys from the current database.
commands.title: FLUSHDB

commands.complexity: Depends on subcommand.
commands.description: A container for hotkeys tracking commands.
commands.title: HOTKEYS

commands.complexity: O(K) where K is the number of hotkeys returned.
commands.description: Returns lists of top K hotkeys depending on metrics chosen in HOTKEYS START command.
commands.title: HOTKEYS GET

commands.description: Release the resources used for hotkey tracking.
commands.title: HOTKEYS RESET

commands.description: Starts hotkeys tracking.
commands.syntax: HOTKEYS START METRICS count [CPU] [NET] [COUNT k] [DURATION seconds] [SAMPLE ratio] [SLOTS count slot [slot ...]]
commands.title: HOTKEYS START

commands.description: Stops hotkeys tracking.
commands.title: HOTKEYS STOP

commands.description: Returns information and statistics about the server.
commands.title: INFO

commands.description: Returns the Unix timestamp of the last successful save to disk.
commands.title: LASTSAVE

commands.description: Returns a human-readable latency analysis report.
commands.title: LATENCY DOCTOR

commands.description: Returns a latency graph for an event.
commands.title: LATENCY GRAPH

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of commands with latency information being retrieved.
commands.description: Returns the cumulative distribution of latencies of a subset or all commands.
commands.title: LATENCY HISTOGRAM

commands.description: Returns timestamp-latency samples for an event.
commands.title: LATENCY HISTORY

commands.description: Returns the latest latency samples for all events.
commands.title: LATENCY LATEST

commands.description: Resets the latency data for one or more events.
commands.title: LATENCY RESET

commands.description: Displays computer art and the Redis version
commands.title: LOLWUT

commands.description: Outputs a memory problems report.
commands.title: MEMORY DOCTOR

commands.complexity: Depends on how much memory is allocated, could be slow
commands.description: Returns the allocator statistics.
commands.title: MEMORY MALLOC-STATS

commands.complexity: Depends on how much memory is allocated, could be slow
commands.description: Asks the allocator to release memory.
commands.title: MEMORY PURGE

commands.description: Returns details about memory usage.
commands.title: MEMORY STATS

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of samples.
commands.description: Estimates the memory usage of a key.
commands.title: MEMORY USAGE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of loaded modules.
commands.description: Returns all loaded modules.
commands.title: MODULE LIST

commands.description: Loads a module.
commands.title: MODULE LOAD

commands.description: Loads a module using extended parameters.
commands.syntax:
MODULE LOADEX path [CONFIG name value [CONFIG name value ...]]
  [ARGS args [args ...]]
commands.title: MODULE LOADEX

commands.description: Unloads a module.
commands.title: MODULE UNLOAD

commands.description: Listens for all requests received by the server in real-time.
commands.title: MONITOR

commands.description: An internal command used in replication.
commands.title: PSYNC

commands.description: An internal command for configuring the replication stream.
commands.title: REPLCONF

commands.description: Configures a server as replica of another, or promotes it to a master.
commands.title: REPLICAOF

commands.complexity: O(1) to create the new key and additional O(N*M) to reconstruct the serialized value, where N is the number of Redis objects composing the value and M their average size. For small string values the time complexity is thus O(1)+O(1*M) where M is small, so simply O(1). However for sorted set values the complexity is O(N*M*log(N)) because inserting values into sorted sets is O(log(N)).
commands.description: An internal command for migrating keys in a cluster.
commands.syntax:
RESTORE-ASKING key ttl serialized-value [REPLACE] [ABSTTL]
  [IDLETIME seconds] [FREQ frequency]
commands.title: RESTORE-ASKING

commands.description: Returns the replication role.
commands.title: ROLE

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of keys in all databases
commands.description: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk.
commands.title: SAVE

commands.complexity: O(N) when saving, where N is the total number of keys in all databases when saving data, otherwise O(1)
commands.description: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk and shuts down the Redis server.
commands.title: SHUTDOWN

commands.description: Sets a Redis server as a replica of another, or promotes it to being a master.
commands.title: SLAVEOF

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of entries returned
commands.description: Returns the slow log's entries.
commands.title: SLOWLOG GET

commands.description: Returns the number of entries in the slow log.
commands.title: SLOWLOG LEN

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of entries in the slowlog
commands.description: Clears all entries from the slow log.
commands.title: SLOWLOG RESET

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the count of clients watching or blocking on keys from both databases.
commands.description: Swaps two Redis databases.
commands.title: SWAPDB

commands.description: An internal command used in replication.
commands.title: SYNC

commands.description: Returns the server time.
commands.title: TIME

commands.complexity: O(1) for each element added, so O(N) to add N elements when the command is called with multiple arguments.
commands.description: Adds one or more members to a set. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: SADD

commands.description: Returns the number of members in a set.
commands.title: SCARD

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.
commands.description: Returns the difference of multiple sets.
commands.title: SDIFF

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.
commands.description: Stores the difference of multiple sets in a key.
commands.title: SDIFFSTORE

commands.complexity: O(N*M) worst case where N is the cardinality of the smallest set and M is the number of sets.
commands.description: Returns the intersect of multiple sets.
commands.title: SINTER

commands.complexity: O(N*M) worst case where N is the cardinality of the smallest set and M is the number of sets.
commands.description: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sets.
commands.title: SINTERCARD

commands.complexity: O(N*M) worst case where N is the cardinality of the smallest set and M is the number of sets.
commands.description: Stores the intersect of multiple sets in a key.
commands.title: SINTERSTORE

commands.description: Determines whether a member belongs to a set.
commands.title: SISMEMBER

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the set cardinality.
commands.description: Returns all members of a set.
commands.title: SMEMBERS

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of elements being checked for membership
commands.description: Determines whether multiple members belong to a set.
commands.title: SMISMEMBER

commands.description: Moves a member from one set to another.
commands.title: SMOVE

commands.complexity: Without the count argument O(1), otherwise O(N) where N is the value of the passed count.
commands.description: Returns one or more random members from a set after removing them. Deletes the set if the last member was popped.
commands.title: SPOP

commands.complexity: Without the count argument O(1), otherwise O(N) where N is the absolute value of the passed count.
commands.description: Get one or multiple random members from a set
commands.title: SRANDMEMBER

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of members to be removed.
commands.description: Removes one or more members from a set. Deletes the set if the last member was removed.
commands.title: SREM

commands.complexity: O(1) for every call. O(N) for a complete iteration, including enough command calls for the cursor to return back to 0. N is the number of elements inside the collection.
commands.description: Iterates over members of a set.
commands.title: SSCAN

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.
commands.description: Returns the union of multiple sets.
commands.title: SUNION

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the total number of elements in all given sets.
commands.description: Stores the union of multiple sets in a key.
commands.title: SUNIONSTORE

commands.complexity: O(K) + O(M*log(N)) where K is the number of provided keys, N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.
commands.description: Removes and returns a member by score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.
commands.syntax: BZMPOP timeout numkeys key [key ...] <MIN | MAX> [COUNT count]
commands.title: BZMPOP

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.
commands.description: Removes and returns the member with the highest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member available otherwise.  Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.
commands.title: BZPOPMAX

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.
commands.description: Removes and returns the member with the lowest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.
commands.title: BZPOPMIN

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) for each item added, where N is the number of elements in the sorted set.
commands.description: Adds one or more members to a sorted set, or updates their scores. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.syntax:
ZADD key [NX | XX] [GT | LT] [CH] [INCR] score member [score member
  ...]
commands.title: ZADD

commands.description: Returns the number of members in a sorted set.
commands.title: ZCARD

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.
commands.description: Returns the count of members in a sorted set that have scores within a range.
commands.title: ZCOUNT

commands.complexity: O(L + (N-K)log(N)) worst case where L is the total number of elements in all the sets, N is the size of the first set, and K is the size of the result set.
commands.description: Returns the difference between multiple sorted sets.
commands.title: ZDIFF

commands.complexity: O(L + (N-K)log(N)) worst case where L is the total number of elements in all the sets, N is the size of the first set, and K is the size of the result set.
commands.description: Stores the difference of multiple sorted sets in a key.
commands.title: ZDIFFSTORE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) where N is the number of elements in the sorted set.
commands.description: Increments the score of a member in a sorted set.
commands.title: ZINCRBY

commands.complexity: O(N*K)+O(M*log(M)) worst case with N being the smallest input sorted set, K being the number of input sorted sets and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.
commands.description: Returns the intersect of multiple sorted sets.
commands.syntax:
ZINTER numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [weight ...]]
  [AGGREGATE <SUM | MIN | MAX>] [WITHSCORES]
commands.title: ZINTER

commands.complexity: O(N*K) worst case with N being the smallest input sorted set, K being the number of input sorted sets.
commands.description: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sorted sets.
commands.title: ZINTERCARD

commands.complexity: O(N*K)+O(M*log(M)) worst case with N being the smallest input sorted set, K being the number of input sorted sets and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.
commands.description: Stores the intersect of multiple sorted sets in a key.
commands.syntax:
ZINTERSTORE destination numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight
  [weight ...]] [AGGREGATE <SUM | MIN | MAX>]
commands.title: ZINTERSTORE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set.
commands.description: Returns the number of members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.
commands.title: ZLEXCOUNT

commands.complexity: O(K) + O(M*log(N)) where K is the number of provided keys, N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.
commands.description: Returns the highest- or lowest-scoring members from one or more sorted sets after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.
commands.title: ZMPOP

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of members being requested.
commands.description: Returns the score of one or more members in a sorted set.
commands.title: ZMSCORE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)*M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.
commands.description: Returns the highest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.
commands.title: ZPOPMAX

commands.complexity: O(log(N)*M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set, and M being the number of elements popped.
commands.description: Returns the lowest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.
commands.title: ZPOPMIN

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of members returned
commands.description: Returns one or more random members from a sorted set.
commands.title: ZRANDMEMBER

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements returned.
commands.description: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes.
commands.syntax:
ZRANGE key start stop [BYSCORE | BYLEX] [REV] [LIMIT offset count]
  [WITHSCORES]
commands.title: ZRANGE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).
commands.description: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.
commands.syntax: ZRANGEBYLEX key min max [LIMIT offset count]
commands.title: ZRANGEBYLEX

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).
commands.description: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores.
commands.syntax: ZRANGEBYSCORE key min max [WITHSCORES] [LIMIT offset count]
commands.title: ZRANGEBYSCORE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements stored into the destination key.
commands.description: Stores a range of members from sorted set in a key.
commands.syntax:
ZRANGESTORE dst src min max [BYSCORE | BYLEX] [REV] [LIMIT offset
  count]
commands.title: ZRANGESTORE

commands.complexity: O(log(N))
commands.description: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by ascending scores.
commands.title: ZRANK

commands.complexity: O(M*log(N)) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements to be removed.
commands.description: Removes one or more members from a sorted set. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
commands.title: ZREM

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements removed by the operation.
commands.description: Removes members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
commands.title: ZREMRANGEBYLEX

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements removed by the operation.
commands.description: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of indexes. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
commands.title: ZREMRANGEBYRANK

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements removed by the operation.
commands.description: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of scores. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.
commands.title: ZREMRANGEBYSCORE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements returned.
commands.description: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes in reverse order.
commands.title: ZREVRANGE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).
commands.description: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range in reverse order.
commands.syntax: ZREVRANGEBYLEX key max min [LIMIT offset count]
commands.title: ZREVRANGEBYLEX

commands.complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).
commands.description: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores in reverse order.
commands.syntax: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE key max min [WITHSCORES] [LIMIT offset count]
commands.title: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE

commands.complexity: O(log(N))
commands.description: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by descending scores.
commands.title: ZREVRANK

commands.complexity: O(1) for every call. O(N) for a complete iteration, including enough command calls for the cursor to return back to 0. N is the number of elements inside the collection.
commands.description: Iterates over members and scores of a sorted set.
commands.title: ZSCAN

commands.description: Returns the score of a member in a sorted set.
commands.title: ZSCORE

commands.complexity: O(N)+O(M*log(M)) with N being the sum of the sizes of the input sorted sets, and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.
commands.description: Returns the union of multiple sorted sets.
commands.syntax:
ZUNION numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [weight ...]]
  [AGGREGATE <SUM | MIN | MAX>] [WITHSCORES]
commands.title: ZUNION

commands.complexity: O(N)+O(M log(M)) with N being the sum of the sizes of the input sorted sets, and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.
commands.description: Stores the union of multiple sorted sets in a key.
commands.syntax:
ZUNIONSTORE destination numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight
  [weight ...]] [AGGREGATE <SUM | MIN | MAX>]
commands.title: ZUNIONSTORE

commands.complexity: O(1) for each message ID processed.
commands.description: Returns the number of messages that were successfully acknowledged by the consumer group member of a stream.
commands.title: XACK

commands.complexity: O(1) for each entry ID processed.
commands.description: Acknowledges and conditionally deletes one or multiple entries for a stream consumer group.
commands.syntax: XACKDEL key group [KEEPREF | DELREF | ACKED] IDS numids id [id ...]
commands.title: XACKDEL

commands.complexity: O(1) when adding a new entry, O(N) when trimming where N being the number of entries evicted.
commands.description: Appends a new message to a stream. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.syntax:
XADD key [NOMKSTREAM] [KEEPREF | DELREF | ACKED]
  [IDMPAUTO producer-id | IDMP producer-id idempotent-id]
  [<MAXLEN | MINID> [= | ~] threshold [LIMIT count]] <* | id>
  field value [field value ...]
commands.title: XADD

commands.complexity: O(1) if COUNT is small.
commands.description: Changes, or acquires, ownership of messages in a consumer group, as if the messages were delivered to as consumer group member.
commands.syntax:
XAUTOCLAIM key group consumer min-idle-time start [COUNT count]
  [JUSTID]
commands.title: XAUTOCLAIM

commands.description: Sets the IDMP configuration parameters for a stream.
commands.title: XCFGSET

commands.complexity: O(log N) with N being the number of messages in the PEL of the consumer group.
commands.description: Changes, or acquires, ownership of a message in a consumer group, as if the message was delivered a consumer group member.
commands.syntax:
XCLAIM key group consumer min-idle-time id [id ...] [IDLE ms]
  [TIME unix-time-milliseconds] [RETRYCOUNT count] [FORCE] [JUSTID]
  [LASTID lastid]
commands.title: XCLAIM

commands.complexity: O(1) for each single item to delete in the stream, regardless of the stream size.
commands.description: Returns the number of messages after removing them from a stream.
commands.title: XDEL

commands.complexity: O(1) for each single item to delete in the stream, regardless of the stream size.
commands.description: Deletes one or multiple entries from the stream.
commands.syntax: XDELEX key [KEEPREF | DELREF | ACKED] IDS numids id [id ...]
commands.title: XDELEX

commands.description: Creates a consumer group.
commands.title: XGROUP CREATE

commands.description: Creates a consumer in a consumer group.
commands.title: XGROUP CREATECONSUMER

commands.description: Deletes a consumer from a consumer group.
commands.title: XGROUP DELCONSUMER

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of entries in the group's pending entries list (PEL).
commands.description: Destroys a consumer group.
commands.title: XGROUP DESTROY

commands.description: Sets the last-delivered ID of a consumer group.
commands.title: XGROUP SETID

commands.description: Returns a list of the consumers in a consumer group.
commands.title: XINFO CONSUMERS

commands.description: Returns a list of the consumer groups of a stream.
commands.title: XINFO GROUPS

commands.description: Returns information about a stream.
commands.title: XINFO STREAM

commands.description: Return the number of messages in a stream.
commands.title: XLEN

commands.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of elements returned, so asking for a small fixed number of entries per call is O(1). O(M), where M is the total number of entries scanned when used with the IDLE filter. When the command returns just the summary and the list of consumers is small, it runs in O(1) time; otherwise, an additional O(N) time for iterating every consumer.
commands.description: Returns the information and entries from a stream consumer group's pending entries list.
commands.syntax: XPENDING key group [[IDLE min-idle-time] start end count [consumer]]
commands.title: XPENDING

commands.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of elements being returned. If N is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with COUNT), you can consider it O(1).
commands.description: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs.
commands.title: XRANGE

commands.complexity: For each stream mentioned: O(M) with M being the number of elements returned. If M is constant (for example, always asking for the first 10 elements with COUNT), you can consider it O(1). On the other side when XREADGROUP blocks, XADD will pay the O(N) time in order to serve the N clients blocked on the stream getting new data.
commands.description: Returns messages from multiple streams with IDs greater than the ones requested. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.
commands.syntax:
XREAD [COUNT count] [BLOCK milliseconds] STREAMS key [key ...] id
  [id ...]
commands.title: XREAD

commands.complexity: For each stream mentioned: O(M) with M being the number of elements returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with COUNT), you can consider it O(1). On the other side when XREADGROUP blocks, XADD will pay the O(N) time in order to serve the N clients blocked on the stream getting new data.
commands.description: Returns new or historical messages from a stream for a consumer in a group. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.
commands.syntax:
XREADGROUP GROUP group consumer [COUNT count] [BLOCK milliseconds]
  [CLAIM min-idle-time] [NOACK] STREAMS key [key ...] id [id ...]
commands.title: XREADGROUP

commands.complexity: O(N) with N being the number of elements returned. If N is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with COUNT), you can consider it O(1).
commands.description: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs in reverse order.
commands.title: XREVRANGE

commands.description: An internal command for replicating stream values.
commands.title: XSETID

commands.complexity: O(N), with N being the number of evicted entries. Constant times are very small however, since entries are organized in macro nodes containing multiple entries that can be released with a single deallocation.
commands.description: Deletes messages from the beginning of a stream.
commands.syntax: XTRIM key <MAXLEN | MINID> [= | ~] threshold [LIMIT count] [KEEPREF | DELREF | ACKED]
commands.title: XTRIM

commands.complexity: O(1). The amortized time complexity is O(1) assuming the appended value is small and the already present value is of any size, since the dynamic string library used by Redis will double the free space available on every reallocation.
commands.description: Appends a string to the value of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: APPEND

commands.description: Decrements the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.
commands.title: DECR

commands.description: Decrements a number from the integer value of a key. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.
commands.title: DECRBY

commands.complexity: O(1) for IFEQ/IFNE, O(N) for IFDEQ/IFDNE where N is the length of the string value.
commands.description: Conditionally removes the specified key based on value or hash digest comparison.
commands.syntax:
DELEX key [IFEQ ifeq-value | IFNE ifne-value | IFDEQ ifdeq-digest |
  IFDNE ifdne-digest]
commands.title: DELEX

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the string value.
commands.description: Returns the hash digest of a string value.
commands.title: DIGEST

commands.description: Returns the string value of a key.
commands.title: GET

commands.description: Returns the string value of a key after deleting the key.
commands.title: GETDEL

commands.description: Returns the string value of a key after setting its expiration time.
commands.syntax:
GETEX key [EX seconds | PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds |
  PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | PERSIST]
commands.title: GETEX

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the returned string. The complexity is ultimately determined by the returned length, but because creating a substring from an existing string is very cheap, it can be considered O(1) for small strings.
commands.description: Returns a substring of the string stored at a key.
commands.title: GETRANGE

commands.description: Returns the previous string value of a key after setting it to a new value.
commands.title: GETSET

commands.description: Increments the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.
commands.title: INCR

commands.description: Increments the integer value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.
commands.title: INCRBY

commands.description: Increment the floating point value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.
commands.title: INCRBYFLOAT

commands.complexity: O(N*M) where N and M are the lengths of s1 and s2, respectively
commands.description: Finds the longest common substring.
commands.title: LCS

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to retrieve.
commands.description: Atomically returns the string values of one or more keys.
commands.title: MGET

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to set.
commands.description: Atomically creates or modifies the string values of one or more keys.
commands.title: MSET

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to set.
commands.description: Atomically sets multiple string keys with a shared expiration in a single operation. Supports flexible argument parsing where condition and expiration flags can appear in any order.
commands.syntax:
MSETEX numkeys key value [key value ...] [NX | XX] [EX seconds |
  PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds |
  PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | KEEPTTL]
commands.title: MSETEX

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of keys to set.
commands.description: Atomically modifies the string values of one or more keys only when all keys don't exist.
commands.title: MSETNX

commands.description: Sets both string value and expiration time in milliseconds of a key. The key is created if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: PSETEX

commands.description: Sets the string value of a key, ignoring its type. The key is created if it doesn't exist.
commands.syntax:
SET key value [NX | XX | IFEQ ifeq-value | IFNE ifne-value |
  IFDEQ ifdeq-digest | IFDNE ifdne-digest] [GET] [EX seconds |
  PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds |
  PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | KEEPTTL]
commands.title: SET

commands.description: Sets the string value and expiration time of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: SETEX

commands.description: Set the string value of a key only when the key doesn't exist.
commands.title: SETNX

commands.complexity: O(1), not counting the time taken to copy the new string in place. Usually, this string is very small so the amortized complexity is O(1). Otherwise, complexity is O(M) with M being the length of the value argument.
commands.description: Overwrites a part of a string value with another by an offset. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.
commands.title: SETRANGE

commands.description: Returns the length of a string value.
commands.title: STRLEN

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the returned string. The complexity is ultimately determined by the returned length, but because creating a substring from an existing string is very cheap, it can be considered O(1) for small strings.
commands.description: Returns a substring from a string value.
commands.title: SUBSTR

commands.description: Adds a suggestion string to an auto-complete suggestion dictionary
commands.title: FT.SUGADD

commands.description: Deletes a string from a suggestion index
commands.title: FT.SUGDEL

commands.description: Gets completion suggestions for a prefix
commands.title: FT.SUGGET

commands.description: Gets the size of an auto-complete suggestion dictionary
commands.title: FT.SUGLEN

commands.complexity: O(N), where N is the number of samples to add
commands.description: Adds one or more observations to a t-digest sketch
commands.title: TDIGEST.ADD

commands.description: Returns, for each input rank, a floating-point estimation of the value with that rank
commands.title: TDIGEST.BYRANK

commands.description: Returns, for each input reverse rank, an estimation of the floating-point value with that reverse rank
commands.title: TDIGEST.BYREVRANK

commands.description: Returns, for each input value, an estimation of the floating-point fraction of (observations smaller than the given value + half the observations equal to the given value)
commands.title: TDIGEST.CDF

commands.description: Allocates memory and initializes a new t-digest sketch
commands.title: TDIGEST.CREATE

commands.description: Returns information and statistics about a t-digest sketch
commands.title: TDIGEST.INFO

commands.description: Returns the maximum observation value from a t-digest sketch
commands.title: TDIGEST.MAX

commands.complexity: O(N*K), where N is the number of centroids and K being the number of input sketches
commands.description: Merges multiple t-digest sketches into a single sketch
commands.title: TDIGEST.MERGE

commands.description: Returns the minimum observation value from a t-digest sketch
commands.title: TDIGEST.MIN

commands.description: Returns, for each input fraction, a floating-point estimation of the value that is smaller than the given fraction of observations
commands.title: TDIGEST.QUANTILE

commands.description: Returns, for each floating-point input value, the estimated rank of the value (the number of observations in the sketch that are smaller than the value + half the number of observations that are equal to the value)
commands.title: TDIGEST.RANK

commands.description: Resets a t-digest sketch (empties the sketch and re-initializes it).
commands.title: TDIGEST.RESET

commands.description: Returns, for each floating-point input value, the estimated reverse rank of the value (the number of observations in the sketch that are larger than the value + half the number of observations that are equal to the value)
commands.title: TDIGEST.REVRANK

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of centroids
commands.description: Returns an estimation of the mean value from the sketch, excluding observation values outside the low and high cutoff quantiles
commands.title: TDIGEST.TRIMMED_MEAN

commands.complexity: O(M) when M is the amount of compaction rules or O(1) with no compaction
commands.description: Append a sample to a time series
commands.syntax:
TS.ADD key timestamp value [RETENTION retentionPeriod]
  [ENCODING <UNCOMPRESSED | COMPRESSED>] [CHUNK_SIZE size]
  [ON_DUPLICATE <BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM>]
  [LABELS label value [label value ...]]
commands.title: TS.ADD

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of labels requested to update
commands.description: Update the retention, chunk size, duplicate policy, and labels of an existing time series
commands.syntax:
TS.ALTER key [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [CHUNK_SIZE size]
  [DUPLICATE_POLICY <BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM>] [LABELS label value
  [label value ...]]
commands.title: TS.ALTER

commands.description: Create a new time series
commands.syntax:
TS.CREATE key [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [ENCODING <UNCOMPRESSED |
  COMPRESSED>] [CHUNK_SIZE size] [DUPLICATE_POLICY <BLOCK | FIRST |
  LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM>] [LABELS label value [label value ...]]
commands.title: TS.CREATE

commands.description: Create a compaction rule.
commands.syntax:
TS.CREATERULE sourceKey destKey AGGREGATION <AVG | FIRST | LAST |
  MIN | MAX | SUM | RANGE | COUNT | STD.P | STD.S | VAR.P | VAR.S |
  TWA> bucketDuration [alignTimestamp]
commands.title: TS.CREATERULE

commands.complexity: O(M) when M is the amount of compaction rules or O(1) with no compaction
commands.description: Decrease the value of the sample with the maximum existing timestamp, or create a new sample with a value equal to the value of the sample with the maximum existing timestamp with a given decrement
commands.syntax:
TS.DECRBY key value [TIMESTAMP timestamp]
  [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [UNCOMPRESSED] [CHUNK_SIZE size]
  [LABELS label value [label value ...]]
commands.title: TS.DECRBY

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the number of data points that will be removed
commands.description: Delete all samples between two timestamps for a given time series
commands.title: TS.DEL

commands.description: Delete a compaction rule
commands.title: TS.DELETERULE

commands.description: Get the sample with the highest timestamp from a given time series
commands.title: TS.GET

commands.complexity: O(M) when M is the amount of compaction rules or O(1) with no compaction
commands.description: Increase the value of the sample with the maximum existing timestamp, or create a new sample with a value equal to the value of the sample with the maximum existing timestamp with a given increment
commands.syntax:
TS.INCRBY key value [TIMESTAMP timestamp]
  [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [UNCOMPRESSED] [CHUNK_SIZE size]
  [LABELS label value [label value ...]]
commands.title: TS.INCRBY

commands.description: Returns information and statistics for a time series
commands.title: TS.INFO

commands.complexity: O(N*M) when N is the amount of series updated and M is the amount of compaction rules or O(N) with no compaction
commands.description: Append new samples to one or more time series
commands.syntax: TS.MADD key timestamp value [key timestamp value ...]
commands.title: TS.MADD

commands.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of time-series that match the filters
commands.description: Get the sample with the highest timestamp from each time series matching a specific filter
commands.syntax:
TS.MGET [LATEST] [WITHLABELS | SELECTED_LABELS label1 [label1 ...]]
  FILTER <l=v | l!=v | l= | l!= | l=(v1,v2,...) | l!=(v1,v2,...)
  [l=v | l!=v | l= | l!= | l=(v1,v2,...) | l!=(v1,v2,...) ...]>
commands.title: TS.MGET

commands.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested ranges
commands.description: Query a range across multiple time series by filters in forward direction
commands.syntax:
TS.MRANGE fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST] [FILTER_BY_TS Timestamp
  [Timestamp ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max] [WITHLABELS |
  SELECTED_LABELS label1 [label1 ...]] [COUNT count] [[ALIGN value]
  AGGREGATION <AVG | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM | RANGE | COUNT
  | STD.P | STD.S | VAR.P | VAR.S | TWA> bucketDuration
  [BUCKETTIMESTAMP] [EMPTY]] FILTER <l=v | l!=v | l= | l!= |
  l=(v1,v2,...) | l!=(v1,v2,...) [l=v | l!=v | l= | l!= |
  l=(v1,v2,...) | l!=(v1,v2,...) ...]> [GROUPBY label REDUCE
  reducer]
commands.title: TS.MRANGE

commands.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested ranges
commands.description: Query a range across multiple time-series by filters in reverse direction
commands.syntax:
TS.MREVRANGE fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST]
  [FILTER_BY_TS Timestamp [Timestamp ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max]
  [WITHLABELS | SELECTED_LABELS label1 [label1 ...]] [COUNT count]
  [[ALIGN value] AGGREGATION <AVG | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM |
  RANGE | COUNT | STD.P | STD.S | VAR.P | VAR.S | TWA>
  bucketDuration [BUCKETTIMESTAMP] [EMPTY]] FILTER <l=v | l!=v | l=
  | l!= | l=(v1,v2,...) | l!=(v1,v2,...) [l=v | l!=v | l= | l!= |
  l=(v1,v2,...) | l!=(v1,v2,...) ...]> [GROUPBY label REDUCE
  reducer]
commands.title: TS.MREVRANGE

commands.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of time-series that match the filters
commands.description: Get all time series keys matching a filter list
commands.title: TS.QUERYINDEX

commands.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested range
commands.description: Query a range in forward direction
commands.syntax:
TS.RANGE key fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST]
  [FILTER_BY_TS Timestamp [Timestamp ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max]
  [COUNT count] [[ALIGN value] AGGREGATION <AVG | FIRST | LAST | MIN
  | MAX | SUM | RANGE | COUNT | STD.P | STD.S | VAR.P | VAR.S | TWA>
  bucketDuration [BUCKETTIMESTAMP] [EMPTY]]
commands.title: TS.RANGE

commands.complexity: O(n/m+k) where n = Number of data points, m = Chunk size (data points per chunk), k = Number of data points that are in the requested range
commands.description: Query a range in reverse direction
commands.syntax:
TS.REVRANGE key fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST]
  [FILTER_BY_TS Timestamp [Timestamp ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max]
  [COUNT count] [[ALIGN value] AGGREGATION <AVG | FIRST | LAST | MIN
  | MAX | SUM | RANGE | COUNT | STD.P | STD.S | VAR.P | VAR.S | TWA>
  bucketDuration [BUCKETTIMESTAMP] [EMPTY]]
commands.title: TS.REVRANGE

commands.complexity: O(n * k) where n is the number of items and k is the depth
commands.description: Adds an item to a Top-k sketch. Multiple items can be added at the same time.
commands.title: TOPK.ADD

commands.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of items
commands.description: Return the count for one or more items in a sketch
commands.title: TOPK.COUNT

commands.complexity: O(n * k * incr) where n is the number of items, k is the depth and incr is the increment
commands.description: Increases the count of one or more items by increment
commands.title: TOPK.INCRBY

commands.description: Returns information about a sketch
commands.title: TOPK.INFO

commands.complexity: O(k) where k is the value of top-k
commands.description: Return the full list of items in the Top-K sketch
commands.title: TOPK.LIST

commands.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of items
commands.description: Checks whether one or more items are in a sketch
commands.title: TOPK.QUERY

commands.description: Initializes a Top-K sketch with specified parameters
commands.title: TOPK.RESERVE

commands.complexity: O(N), when N is the number of queued commands
commands.description: Discards a transaction.
commands.title: DISCARD

commands.complexity: Depends on commands in the transaction
commands.description: Executes all commands in a transaction.
commands.title: EXEC

commands.description: Starts a transaction.
commands.title: MULTI

commands.description: Forgets about watched keys of a transaction.
commands.title: UNWATCH

commands.complexity: O(1) for every key.
commands.description: Monitors changes to keys to determine the execution of a transaction.
commands.title: WATCH

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) for each element added, where N is the number of elements in the vector set.
commands.description: Add a new element to a vector set, or update its vector if it already exists.
commands.syntax:
VADD key [REDUCE dim] (FP32 | VALUES num) vector element [CAS] [NOQUANT | Q8 | BIN]
  [EF build-exploration-factor] [SETATTR attributes] [M numlinks]
commands.title: VADD

commands.description: Return the number of elements in a vector set.
commands.title: VCARD

commands.description: Return the dimension of vectors in the vector set.
commands.title: VDIM

commands.description: Return the vector associated with an element.
commands.title: VEMB

commands.description: Retrieve the JSON attributes of elements.
commands.title: VGETATTR

commands.description: Return information about a vector set.
commands.title: VINFO

commands.description: Check if an element exists in a vector set.
commands.title: VISMEMBER

commands.description: Return the neighbors of an element at each layer in the HNSW graph.
commands.title: VLINKS

commands.complexity: O(N) where N is the absolute value of the count argument.
commands.description: Return one or multiple random members from a vector set.
commands.title: VRANDMEMBER

commands.complexity: O(log(K)+M) where K is the number of elements in the start prefix, and M is the number of elements returned. In practical terms, the command is just O(M)
commands.description: Return elements in a lexicographical range
commands.title: VRANGE

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) for each element removed, where N is the number of elements in the vector set.
commands.description: Remove an element from a vector set.
commands.title: VREM

commands.description: Associate or remove the JSON attributes of elements.
commands.title: VSETATTR

commands.complexity: O(log(N)) where N is the number of elements in the vector set.
commands.description: Return elements by vector similarity.
commands.syntax:
VSIM key (ELE | FP32 | VALUES num) (vector | element) [WITHSCORES] [WITHATTRIBS] [COUNT num]
  [EPSILON delta] [EF search-exploration-factor] [FILTER expression] [FILTER-EF max-filtering-effort]
  [TRUTH] [NOTHREAD]
commands.title: VSIM
